page: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-80 is a memory-optimized server designed for workloads requiring massive memory capacity relative to compute resources. It features 80 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 40 physical cores on an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server is equipped with 1922 GB of system memory, providing approximately 24.02 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core performance is positioned in the bottom 10% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and compression tests. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests, achieve average tier results. Memory bandwidth tests also show average performance, though the Passmark memory score falls into the bottom 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers high memory density, making it suitable for consolidating memory-bound workloads that do not require high single-threaded CPU performance. It is well-suited for large in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-80 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server configured with 80 dedicated vCPUs and 1922 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 24.02 GB per vCPU. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz, this server does not include local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark data reveals a performance profile characterized by poor single-core execution but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. Consequently, this instance is designed for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-intensive tasks. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications that require massive memory footprints to process large datasets without relying on local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-80 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 80 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1922 GB of RAM, designed for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized server featuring massive RAM capacity for memory-bound enterprise applications and large in-memory databases.

High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 24.02 GB per core
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Designed for large-memory workloads like in-memory databases
